recommendations: define and run a minimum MCP smoke test suite (import + instantiate each exposed endpoint) because test status is empty
add contract tests that pin input/output schemas for high-value endpoints like `MMM`
`MultidimensionalMMM`
and `build_mmm_from_yaml`
introduce endpoint allowlisting and clearer naming/aliasing (e.g.
preserve class case or add snake_case aliases) to reduce ambiguity in generated tool names
add robust error mapping in the adapter layer (convert PyMC/pydantic/internal exceptions into structured MCP errors with actionable messages)
implement timeout and resource guards for long-running Bayesian sampling calls to prevent MCP server lockups
add lazy imports for heavy modules (PyMC/plotting/streamlit) to improve MCP startup latency and reliability
generate task-focused MCP wrappers instead of raw class exposure (e.g.
fit/predict/optimize tools) to reduce intrusiveness risk in a complex codebase
pin and validate dependency compatibility across `pyproject.toml`
`environment.yml`
and `mcp_output/requirements.txt` to avoid environment drift
add CI workflow for `mcp_output` (lint
type-check
smoke tests
packaging check) integrated with existing GitHub Actions
add structured logging and tracing IDs in `mcp_service.py` for observability and easier debugging
add authentication/authorization and rate limiting if MCP is used beyond local trusted environments
create reproducible benchmark scenarios (small synthetic MMM/CLV datasets) and track latency/memory per endpoint
add fallback behavior for DeepWiki analysis failure (degrade gracefully with cached/static summaries)
improve README_MCP with concrete examples per endpoint and known heavy-operation caveats
add versioned API metadata in MCP responses (plugin version + upstream `pymc_marketing` version) for compatibility tracking

success_factors: Workflow completed end-to-end with status=success across all planned nodes (download, analysis, env, generate, run, review, finalize).
Import-based adapter strategy was feasible (import_feasibility=0.87), enabling direct exposure of key pymc-marketing classes/functions.
MCP plugin started healthy over stdio, indicating generated service wiring (entrypoint, plugin package, runtime deps) is functionally valid.
Repository structure and package discovery were strong (303 files scanned; major domains MMM/CLV/customer_choice/data identified), supporting meaningful endpoint generation.
failure_reasons: Quality/validation gap: original project tests were not executed meaningfully (passed=false, empty details, test_files=[] despite reported 100% coverage), reducing confidence in compatibility.
DeepWiki enrichment failed, so contextual documentation intelligence was unavailable and had to fall back to direct analysis.
Observability and performance metrics were effectively missing (zeros/empty), limiting runtime diagnosis.

node_performance.download_time: Completed successfully; exact per-node timing unavailable. Zip fallback import worked and did not block downstream stages.
node_performance.analysis_time: Completed successfully on a large, complex codebase; identified core modules and risks. DeepWiki sub-step failed but analysis continued via fallback.
node_performance.generation_time: Completed successfully; generated MCP scaffold and adapter files with import-mode endpoint mapping.
node_performance.test_time: MCP health test passed (healthy startup), but comprehensive functional/contract testing is absent; test evidence is currently shallow.

resource_usage.memory_efficiency: Undetermined due to missing telemetry (reported 0 MB). For Bayesian workloads, memory risk is likely medium-high during fit/sampling endpoints.
resource_usage.cpu_efficiency: Undetermined due to missing telemetry (reported 0%). CPU-intensive inference paths (PyMC sampling/optimization) likely dominate under real load.
resource_usage.disk_usage: Generated plugin footprint appears small, but size stats are missing (0 values). Main disk risk remains model artifacts/logging if enabled later.
